questions.prompt: Choose the correct article: "Please bring ___ umbrella — it is raining."
questions.explanation: Umbrella starts with the vowel sound /ʌ/, so it takes an: an umbrella. The article a is used before consonant sounds, and the would only work if a specific, already-known umbrella were meant. First mention of an unspecified object gets a or an depending on the sound.

questions.prompt: What is the correct plural of "bus"?
questions.explanation: Bus ends in -s, so it takes -es in the plural: bus → buses. Just adding another -s ("buss") would make the ending hard to pronounce, the -ies rule applies only to nouns ending in consonant + y, and bus without any ending stays singular.

questions.prompt: Fill the gaps: "I found ___ wallet in the street. I gave ___ wallet to the police."
questions.explanation: The first mention of something unknown uses a: I found a wallet. The second mention switches to the because now both speaker and listener know which wallet: I gave the wallet to the police. This a → the shift is the core article pattern.

questions.prompt: What is the correct plural of "foot"?
questions.explanation: Foot is an irregular noun that changes its vowel in the plural: foot → feet (like tooth → teeth, goose → geese). "foots" wrongly applies the regular -s rule, "feets" adds -s to a form that is already plural, and "footes" is an invented ending.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence about rice in general.

options.text: A rice is popular in many countries.
An rice is popular in many countries.
Rice is popular in many countries.
The rices are popular in many countries.

questions.explanation: Rice is an uncountable noun used here as a general statement, so it takes no article and has no plural form: Rice is popular in many countries. You cannot say a rice or an rice (uncountable nouns never take a/an), and "the rices" would wrongly suggest a countable plural.

options.text: There are six women in the room.
I need a information about the bus.

questions.explanation: The error is (d): information is an uncountable noun and cannot take the article a — say I need some information or I need information. Sentences (a), (b) and (c) are all correct: the irregular plural women, an engineer (vowel sound /e/), and the irregular plural children.
